003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024/**
025 * Line-driven {@link Iterator} over the events in a <c>text/event-stream</c>.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Wraps a {@link Reader} the caller owns and dispatches one {@link SseEvent} at a time using the
029 * WHATWG parse state machine (<a class="doclink" href="https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html">HTML §9.2</a>).
030 * Designed for long-lived SSE streams that outlive any reasonable per-call buffer.
031 *
032 * <h5 class='section'>Why a separate class:</h5>
033 * <p>
034 * No other Juneau marshaller ships a separate iterator-style helper. SSE warrants the deviation
035 * because SSE responses are open until the client disconnects or the server pushes its last event;
036 * forcing callers through the eager {@code List<SseEvent>} path would defeat the entire point of
037 * using SSE.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
040 * <p class='bjava'>
041 *    <jk>try</jk> (SseEventReader <jv>r</jv> = <jk>new</jk> SseEventReader(<jv>responseReader</jv>)) {
042 *       <jk>while</jk> (<jv>r</jv>.hasNext()) {
043 *          SseEvent <jv>e</jv> = <jv>r</jv>.next();
044 *          <jc>// Dispatch...</jc>
045 *       }
046 *    }
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
050 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
054 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SseBasics">SSE Basics</a>
055 * </ul>
056 */
057@SuppressWarnings({
058   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for readLine / nextEvent — they encode the SSE lexical state machine
059   "java:S115", // Match AssertionUtils arg-name style used throughout Juneau.
060   "resource" // in is the caller-owned Reader (see class Javadoc); this iterator reads from it but does not own its lifecycle.
061})
062public class SseEventReader implements Iterator<SseEvent>, Closeable {
063
064   private static final String ARG_in = "in";
065
066   /** UTF-8 BOM character that must be stripped exactly once from the start of an SSE stream. */
067   private static final int BOM = 0xFEFF;
068
069   private final Reader in;
070
071   private boolean bomChecked;
072   private boolean eof;
073   private SseEvent peeked;
074
075   private final StringBuilder dataBuf = new StringBuilder();
076   private String eventName;
077   private String lastEventId;
078   private Long retryValue;
079   private boolean haveAnyField;
080
081   private int pendingChar = -1;
082
083   /**
084    * Constructor.
085    *
086    * @param in The reader to consume events from. Must not be <jk>null</jk>. Caller retains ownership;
087    *    {@link #close()} closes this reader.
088    */
089   @SuppressWarnings({
090      "java:S2095" // Reader ownership is transferred to this Closeable and released by close().
091   })
092   public SseEventReader(Reader in) {
093      this.in = assertArgNotNull(ARG_in, in);
094   }
095
096   @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
097   public boolean hasNext() {
098      if (peeked != null)
099         return true;
100      if (eof)
101         return false;
102      try {
103         peeked = nextEvent();
104      } catch (IOException e) {
105         throw new UncheckedIOException(e);
106      }
107      return peeked != null;
108   }
109
110   @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
111   public SseEvent next() {
112      if (!hasNext())
113         throw new NoSuchElementException();
114      var out = peeked;
115      peeked = null;
116      return out;
117   }
118
119   @Override /* Overridden from Closeable */
120   public void close() throws IOException {
121      in.close();
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Drains the remaining events into a list, then closes this reader.
126    *
127    * @return The list of events in order.
128    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while reading.
129    */
130   public List<SseEvent> toList() throws IOException {
131      var out = new ArrayList<SseEvent>();
132      try {
133         while (hasNext())
134            out.add(next());
135      } catch (UncheckedIOException e) {
136         throw e.getCause();
137      } finally {
138         close();
139      }
140      return out;
141   }
142
143   private SseEvent nextEvent() throws IOException {
144      while (true) {
145         var line = readLine();
146         if (line == null) {
147            eof = true;
148            if (haveAnyField)
149               return dispatch();
150            return null;
151         }
152         if (line.isEmpty()) {
153            if (haveAnyField)
154               return dispatch();
155            continue;
156         }
157         processField(line);
158      }
159   }
160
161   private SseEvent dispatch() {
162      var out = new SseEvent();
163      out.setEvent(eventName);
164      if (!dataBuf.isEmpty()) {
165         if (dataBuf.charAt(dataBuf.length() - 1) == '\n')
166            dataBuf.deleteCharAt(dataBuf.length() - 1);
167         out.setData(dataBuf.toString());
168      }
169      out.setId(lastEventId);
170      out.setRetry(retryValue);
171      resetEventBuffers();
172      return out;
173   }
174
175   private void resetEventBuffers() {
176      dataBuf.setLength(0);
177      eventName = null;
178      lastEventId = null;
179      retryValue = null;
180      haveAnyField = false;
181   }
182
183   private void processField(String line) {
184      if (line.charAt(0) == ':') {
185         haveAnyField = true;
186         return;
187      }
188      String fieldName;
189      String fieldValue;
190      var colon = line.indexOf(':');
191      if (colon < 0) {
192         fieldName = line;
193         fieldValue = "";
194      } else {
195         fieldName = line.substring(0, colon);
196         fieldValue = line.substring(colon + 1);
197         if (!fieldValue.isEmpty() && fieldValue.charAt(0) == ' ')
198            fieldValue = fieldValue.substring(1);
199      }
200      haveAnyField = true;
201      applyField(fieldName, fieldValue);
202   }
203
204   private void applyField(String fieldName, String fieldValue) {
205      switch (fieldName) {
206         case "event":
207            eventName = fieldValue;
208            break;
209         case "data":
210            dataBuf.append(fieldValue).append('\n');
211            break;
212         case "id":
213            if (fieldValue.indexOf('\u0000') < 0)
214               lastEventId = fieldValue;
215            break;
216         case "retry":
217            retryValue = parseRetry(fieldValue);
218            break;
219         default:
220            break;
221      }
222   }
223
224   private static Long parseRetry(String s) {
225      if (s.isEmpty())
226         return null;
227      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
228         var c = s.charAt(i);
229         if (c < '0' || c > '9')
230            return null;
231      }
232      try {
233         return Long.valueOf(s);
234      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
235         return null;
236      }
237   }
238
239   @SuppressWarnings({
240      "java:S3776" // SSE line parsing is a deliberate lexical state machine.
241   })
242   private String readLine() throws IOException {
243      var sb = new StringBuilder();
244      int c;
245      var sawAny = false;
246      while (true) {
247         if (pendingChar != -1) {
248            c = pendingChar;
249            pendingChar = -1;
250         } else {
251            c = in.read();
252         }
253         if (c == -1) {
254            if (!sawAny)
255               return null;
256            return sb.toString();
257         }
258         if (!bomChecked) {
259            bomChecked = true;
260            if (c == BOM)
261               continue;
262         }
263         sawAny = true;
264         if (c == '\n')
265            return sb.toString();
266         if (c == '\r') {
267            var next = in.read();
268            if (next != -1 && next != '\n')
269               pendingChar = next;
270            return sb.toString();
271         }
272         sb.append((char) c);
273      }
274   }
275}
